threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still stop at every traffic light, instead of just sailing through when the coast is clear. It’s a rookie mistake, mate.
Last night on the 35, some bloke serenaded us with 90s boy band hits. You never know what you'll get after midnight in Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I realized I lived here was the day I saw an impromptu open-air library springing up in a park, books just left on a bench.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You can find a spontaneous dance party in the U-Bahn at 2 AM, complete with a DJ and a crowd of locals. Only in Kreuzberg!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they order an egg and chorizo burrito without asking for extra salsa—it's all about the perfect ratio.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a bus where a toddler was leading an impromptu sing-along. Even the driver cracked a smile, but the rest of us were just stuck in traffic.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think Gràcia is all about Gaudí; many miss the local murals that tell real stories of the neighborhood's past.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by their bewilderment at how many people will squeeze onto a single bus during rush hour. It’s like a sardine can!

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it the Overground instead of the Orange Line. The locals know it’s a badge of honour to hate the Tube.
Saw a lad on the 50 last night trying to convince the driver he was an 'Uber for buses.' Got off in front of the Ritzy. Classic Brixton vibe.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When you hear a neighbor serenading the streets with an old accordion, and the sound floats through the open window while you sip your coffee.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a kebab shop that accepts your payment in laughter instead of money—just bring your best joke!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You’ll see locals double-checking if the burrito has cilantro; tourists just dive in, oblivious to the flavor betrayal.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a full tuxedo boarding the 19, cradling a champagne bottle. Only in SF would the Muni be the ride to the gala.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all gentrified cafes, but the real vibe hides in the bare-bones bars where old locals still gather for tapas and cards.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they stand staring at the metro map, trying to make sense of all the lines and colors. They haven’t yet learned that the real map is in their phone.
